findings.summary: requests is used in core infrastructure but declared only as an optional LLM group dependency
evidence: infrastructure/llm/utils/ollama.py:26: `import requests` (top-level, not guarded) — this module is reachable without the llm optional group
infrastructure/publishing/api.py:9: `import requests` (top-level) — publishing is not behind an optional group

pyproject.toml:57-59: requests is declared only under [dependency-groups] llm, not in [project] dependencies — callers of publishing.api or llm.utils.ollama will fail at import if only core deps are installed
findings.suggestion: Either move requests into [project] dependencies alongside numpy/matplotlib/pypdf/pyyaml, or guard the import in each module with try/except ImportError and raise a clear error at call time (following the _optional_deps.py pattern already established for psutil).

findings.summary: SystemHealthChecker._check_dependencies hardcodes a dep list that diverges from pyproject.toml
evidence: health_check.py:176-182: critical_deps = ['numpy', 'matplotlib', 'requests', 'pyyaml', 'psutil'] — includes requests and psutil as 'critical' but both are optional in pyproject.toml; pypdf (a declared core dep) is absent from the list
This creates a false 'missing' signal for psutil/requests on installs without optional groups, and misses pypdf failures entirely
findings.suggestion: Drive the health check dep list from pyproject.toml at runtime (e.g., read [project].dependencies via importlib.metadata) or at minimum align the hardcoded list with actual [project] dependencies and demote requests/psutil to the existing optional_deps check block.

findings.summary: infrastructure/reporting/suite_runner.py has no dedicated test file despite being a critical execution path
evidence: tests/infra_tests/reporting/ contains 12 test files covering pipeline_reporter, coverage_parser, executive_reporter, html_templates, etc., but no test_suite_runner.py file exists
suite_runner.py is the path that actually invokes pytest as a subprocess and streams output — errors here silently swallow test failures rather than propagating them
findings.suggestion: Add tests/infra_tests/reporting/test_suite_runner.py covering at minimum: successful pytest invocation on a trivial temp test file, failed invocation returning non-zero exit code, and coverage file path resolution. Use real temp pytest runs (no mocks) per project policy.

findings.summary: _run_infrastructure_tests is a one-line private wrapper that only calls run_infrastructure_tests with no added policy
evidence: pipeline.py:396-398: `def _run_infrastructure_tests(self) -> bool: / """Run infrastructure tests.""" / return self.run_infrastructure_tests()` — the private method adds no logic and exists solely to satisfy a stage-dispatch table that could reference the public method directly
This is the exact pass-through wrapper anti-pattern flagged by ai_generated_debt: private wrapper with docstring restating its one-line body
findings.suggestion: Replace the `StageSpec("Infrastructure Tests", self._run_infrastructure_tests)` entry in pipeline.py:126 with `StageSpec("Infrastructure Tests", self.run_infrastructure_tests)` and delete the `_run_infrastructure_tests` method.

findings.summary: Multi-line Args/Returns docstrings on trivial one-to-three-line methods throughout logging_progress.py and logging_formatters.py
evidence: logging_progress.py:123-130: Spinner.__init__ has a 4-line Args docstring for message/stream/delay — parameters are self-documenting from their names and types
logging_progress.py:252-257: StreamingProgress.update has Args block for `increment` and `custom_message` on a 3-line body
logging_formatters.py:19-27: JSONFormatter.format has Args/Returns docblock for a standard logging.Formatter override where the signature is defined by the base class
logging_formatters.py:60-68: TemplateFormatter.format repeats the same Args/Returns pattern for a method with identical shape
findings.suggestion: Collapse multi-line Args/Returns docstrings for methods whose parameter names and type annotations already convey the information. A single-line summary is sufficient for trivial methods (e.g. `"""Format log record as JSON."""`). Reserve Args/Returns blocks for non-obvious parameters or non-obvious return shapes.

findings.summary: The logging subsystem is fragmented across five sibling files in infrastructure/core/ with ambiguous boundaries
evidence: logging_utils.py re-exports format_duration and format_error_with_suggestions from logging_helpers.py via its __all__, making logging_utils.py a hub that consumers import from even when the implementation lives elsewhere — confirmed by pipeline_reporter.py:16 importing format_duration from logging_utils
logging_progress.py imports from logging_helpers.py (format_duration) and logging_constants.py — three-hop import chain for a single utility function

Five files (constants, formatters, helpers, progress, utils) all named logging_* in the same flat directory; a single infrastructure/core/logging/ subpackage would make the boundary explicit and navigable
findings.suggestion: Consolidate the five logging_* modules into a infrastructure/core/logging/ subpackage with an __init__.py that re-exports the public surface (get_logger, setup_logger, log_operation, etc.). This moves the fragmented logging concern under one directory boundary without changing any external import paths if __init__.py re-exports are maintained.
